name: ISP Customer Support Agent
description:
A full-service inbound support agent for internet service providers. Verifies account identity, then routes to the correct resolution path: outage reporting with area-wide fault detection, connectivity troubleshooting with automatic fault ticket creation, billing dispute logging, or plan upgrades and downgrades.

Flow: Entry → Collect account number → Account verification (API) → Intent triage → [Outage: check area outage status → area outage confirmed (terminal) | connection troubleshoot → raise fault ticket (API) → ticket raised (terminal) | Billing: fetch billing summary → billing Q&A → log dispute (API) → dispute raised | Plan change: present plans → collect desired plan → process plan change (API) → confirmed | Escalation handoff].

Facts collected: account_number, customer_name, verified, subscription_status, current_plan, outage_type, troubleshoot_result, fault_ticket_id, billing_query_type, dispute_amount, desired_plan.
Tools required: isp.verify_account, isp.check_area_outage, isp.raise_fault_ticket, billing.fetch_summary, billing.log_dispute, plans.get_available, plans.process_change.

default_agent_settings.system_prompt: You are a customer support agent for an internet service provider. You speak over voice, so keep your responses short and clear. Start by verifying the customer's account using their account number. Once verified, understand their issue: connection problems, billing questions, or interest in changing their plan. For connectivity issues: first check whether there is a known area outage. If no outage, walk them through basic troubleshooting and raise a fault ticket if the issue persists. For billing: fetch their current bill summary, answer questions, and log a dispute if they believe there is an error. For plan changes: present available plans and process the change if they decide. If you cannot resolve the issue, escalate to a human agent.

required_tools.description: Retrieves the billing summary for the customer's account.
Logs a billing dispute for follow-up by the billing team.
Changes the customer's broadband plan. Destructive — verify the new plan with the customer first.
Looks up a customer's account by account number. Required to verify identity before any account-scoped action.
Checks whether there is an active outage in the customer's service area.
Raises a fault ticket in the operations system for follow-up by a field engineer.

A full-service inbound support agent for internet service providers. Verifies account identity, then routes to the correct resolution path: outage reporting with area-wide fault detection, connectivity troubleshooting with automatic fault ticket creation, billing dispute logging, or plan upgrades and downgrades.

Flow: Entry → Collect account number → Account verification (API) → Intent triage → [Outage: check area outage status → area outage confirmed (terminal) | connection troubleshoot → raise fault ticket (API) → ticket raised (terminal) | Billing: fetch billing summary → billing Q&A → log dispute (API) → dispute raised | Plan change: present plans → collect desired plan → process plan change (API) → confirmed | Escalation handoff].

Facts collected: account_number, customer_name, verified, subscription_status, current_plan, outage_type, troubleshoot_result, fault_ticket_id, billing_query_type, dispute_amount, desired_plan.
Tools required: isp.verify_account, isp.check_area_outage, isp.raise_fault_ticket, billing.fetch_summary, billing.log_dispute, plans.get_available, plans.process_change.

steps.name: Entry
steps.say: Hello! Welcome to customer support. I can help with connection issues, billing questions, or plan changes. Could you please provide your account number to get started?

transitions.label: We need the next detail to continue.

steps.name: Collect Account Number
steps.say: Ask for the user's account number naturally and make clear it is needed to look up the customer account, line status, or bill details.

fact_requirements.name: account_number
fact_requirements.purpose: to look up the customer account, line status, or bill details

transitions.label: We already have the account number.
We are still working through the current step.
Action code success happened.
Action code not found happened.
Action code error happened.
We are still working through the current step.

response_policy.direct_answer_prompt: I can help with connection issues, billing, plan changes, or connect you with our team.

when.description: Customer reports no internet, slow speeds, intermittent drops, Wi-Fi not working, or network outage. Phrases: ‘internet is down’, ‘connection dropping’, ‘no signal’, ‘very slow’.

transitions.label: The user is asking about connectivity issue.

when.description: Customer has a question about their bill, payment, charges, invoice, direct debit, dispute, overcharge, or account balance.

transitions.label: The user is asking about billing query.

when.description: Customer wants to upgrade, downgrade, change, or cancel their broadband plan. Asks about available plans, pricing tiers, or contract terms.

transitions.label: The user is asking about plan change.

when.description: Customer is frustrated, demanding a manager, threatening to cancel, or has an issue that couldn’t be resolved previously.

transitions.label: The user is asking about escalation request.

when.description: Customer is satisfied or ending the conversation.

transitions.label: The user is asking about close.
We are still working through the current step.
Action code outage active happened.
Action code clear happened.
Action code error happened.
We are still working through the current step.

when.description: Customer confirms internet is working again after troubleshooting.

transitions.label: The user is asking about issue resolved.

when.description: Customer wants to raise a formal fault report.

transitions.label: The user is asking about wants fault ticket.

when.description: No lights on modem, router broken, or confirmed hardware fault.

transitions.label: The user is asking about issue persists hardware.

when.description: Basic troubleshooting didn’t help. Issue appears configuration or account-level.

transitions.label: The user is asking about issue persists software.
We are still working through the current step.
Action code success happened.
Action code error happened.
We are still working through the current step.
Action code success happened.
Action code error happened.
We are still working through the current step.

when.description: Customer believes they were overcharged, sees an unexpected charge, or is questioning the accuracy of their bill.

transitions.label: The user is asking about billing dispute.
The user is asking about billing resolved.

when.description: Customer pivots from billing discussion to changing their plan.

transitions.label: The user is asking about wants plan change.
The user is asking about close.
We are still working through the current step.
Action code success happened.
Action code error happened.
We are still working through the current step.

when.description: Customer has named a specific plan they want to switch to.

transitions.label: The user is asking about plan selected.

when.description: Customer is threatening to cancel their service.

transitions.label: The user is asking about wants cancellation.
The user is asking about close.
We are still working through the current step.

steps.name: Collect Desired Plan
steps.say: Ask for the user's desired plan naturally and make clear it is needed to recommend or confirm the right plan change.

fact_requirements.name: desired_plan
fact_requirements.purpose: to recommend or confirm the right plan change

transitions.label: We already have the desired plan.
We are still working through the current step.

action_config.code:
response = crm(action="change_plan", account_number=vars.get("account_number"), current_plan=vars.get("current_plan"), desired_plan=vars.get("desired_plan"))
result = normalize_tool_result(response, ["success", "error"])

transitions.label: Action code success happened.
Action code error happened.
We are still working through the current step.

steps.name: Escalation Handoff
steps.say: I understand your frustration. Let me connect you with a member of our support team who can give this the attention it deserves.

handoff.summary: I understand your frustration. Let me connect you with a member of our support team who can give this the attention it deserves.

limitations: I only use configured support systems and account data.
